clean_story:
Now I will teach you You.

You is the one who sees through these eyes, feels hunger in this belly, lifts this arm to throw spear. Not the body—body is like cave, but You are fire inside that warms it, moves it, makes it hunt or hide. When rock cuts foot, You scream; when sun warms skin, You laugh—always You, never other. If you close eyes and listen, You hear thoughts like wind in trees, but wind is yours alone. Others see your hands, but only You taste berry’s sweet, fear bear’s growl, dream of mammoth chase. Point at chest, say "I"—that is You.
